<system-reminder># System Information

Operating System: linux

Current Working Directory: /
Current Time: Tue Jun 09 2026 04:59:51 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)

# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

# RedwoodSDK: Multi-Room Realtime Chat ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. Build a chat application whose room IDs are taken from the URL path and whose chat messages are kept in real-time sync per room using `useSyncedState` with a room scope. ## Requirements * Update `wrangler.jsonc` to register the `SyncedStateServer` Durable Object under the binding name `SYNCED_STATE_SERVER` (with the required SQLite migration including `"new_sqlite_classes": ["SyncedStateServer"]`). * In `src/worker.tsx`, re-export `SyncedStateServer` and register `syncedStateRoutes(() => env.SYNCED_STATE_SERVER)` inside `defineApp([...])`. * Create a client component at `src/app/components/ChatRoom.tsx` (`"use client"`) that accepts a `roomId` prop and calls `useSyncedState([], "messages", roomId)`. It must render: * A scrollable list where each message appears inside an element with attribute `data-testid="chat-message"`. * An `` for typing. * A `` button that appends the input's value to the synced array (and clears the input). * A `` button that resets messages to `[]`. * Implement `GET /chat/:roomId` as a server route that renders a page including `

Room:

` and the `ChatRoom` client component with the `roomId` parameter from the URL. ## Acceptance Criteria * Project path: /home/user/myapp * Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 * Port: 5173 * `wrangler.jsonc` contains the `SYNCED_STATE_SERVER` durable object binding and the `SyncedStateServer` SQLite migration. * `GET /chat/general` returns HTML containing `

Room: general

` and the visible label `Send`. * `GET /chat/redwood` returns HTML containing `

Room: redwood

`. * Browser verification on http://localhost:5173/chat/general: after clicking `Clear`, typing `hello redwood`, clicking `Send`, the message text `hello redwood` appears inside a `data-testid="chat-message"` element. Then on http://localhost:5173/chat/other, after clicking `Clear`, no chat message with text `hello redwood` is visible (state is room-scoped).
Let me start by exploring the pre-installed RedwoodSDK project to understand its structure, then implement the required changes.

output.content:
export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

export declare const createSyncedStateHook: (options?: CreateSyncedStateHookOptions) => <T>(initialValue: T, key: string, roomId?: string | undefined) => [T, Setter<T>];
export declare const useSyncedState: <T>(initialValue: T, key: string, roomId?: string | undefined) => [T, Setter<T>];
export type { SyncedStateClient, SyncedStateStatus, StatusChangeCallback, } from "./client-core.js";
declare function reconnect(endpoint: string, deadClient: SyncedStateClient): void;
export declare const getSyncedStateClient: (endpoint?: string) => SyncedStateClient;
export declare const setSyncedStateClientForTesting: (client: SyncedStateClient | null, endpoint?: string) => void;
* Builds a `useSyncedState` hook configured with optional endpoint and hook overrides.
return function useSyncedState(initialValue, key, roomId = options.roomId) {
// Re-export useSyncedState (no circular dependency since useSyncedState imports from client-core, not client)
// Stub window so relative URLs get normalized inside getSyncedStateClient
// This mirrors what useSyncedState.ts does: register listener with
// the same (relative) string it passes to getSyncedStateClient.
// ws:// or wss:// URL so the same key is used by getSyncedStateClient,
// createSyncedStateHook({ onStatusChange })) are tracked as two separate
// Evict the dead client so getSyncedStateClient creates a fresh one
// Use getSyncedStateClient which now handles caching via Map

input.result:
The multi-room realtime chat application is implemented and running at http://localhost:5173. ## Changes made ### `wrangler.jsonc` - Added `durable_objects.bindings` entry with `name: "SYNCED_STATE_SERVER"` and `class_name: "SyncedStateServer"` - Added `migrations` array with tag `"v1"` and `new_sqlite_classes: ["SyncedStateServer"]` ### `src/worker.tsx` - Imported `SyncedStateServer` and `syncedStateRoutes` from `rwsdk/use-synced-state/worker` - Re-exported `SyncedStateServer` (required by Cloudflare DO runtime) - Spread `syncedStateRoutes(() => env.SYNCED_STATE_SERVER)` into `defineApp([...])` - Added `route("/chat/:roomId", ...)` that renders `

Room: {roomId}

` and the `` client component ### `src/app/components/ChatRoom.tsx` (new file) - `"use client"` directive at the top - Accepts `roomId` prop; calls `useSyncedState([], "messages", roomId)` for room-scoped sync - Renders a scrollable message list where each message has `data-testid="chat-message"` - `` with controlled value - `` — appends input value to synced messages array and clears the input - `` — resets messages to `[]` Verified: - `GET /chat/general` returns HTML containing `

Room: general

` and the `Send` button label ✓ - `GET /chat/redwood` returns HTML containing `

Room: redwood

` ✓ - State is scoped per `roomId` via the third argument to `useSyncedState`
